Class BlockMapLogic

java.lang.Object
tailFinder.model.map.BlockMapLogic
public class BlockMapLogic extends Object
Manages the game world map and block data. Loads map patterns from resource files based on difficulty level. Provides methods to access and modify the game world at any position. Converts byte values to corresponding block types (solid blocks, passable blocks, enemies).

    • map

  • Constructor Details

    • BlockMapLogic

      public BlockMapLogic(int difficult)
      Initializes the BlockMapLogic with a specific difficulty level. Loads the appropriate map pattern file and creates the game world. Easy and Normal difficulties use 12x60 maps, Hard uses 11x10 map.
      Parameters:
      difficult - difficulty level (0=Easy, 1=Normal, 2=Hard)

  • Method Details

    • createMap

      public byte[][] createMap(int difficult)
      Creates a new map from the map pattern file corresponding to the difficulty level. Reads byte values from the resource file and populates the map array.
      Parameters:
      difficult - difficulty level (0=Easy, 1=Normal, 2=Hard)
      Returns:
      the newly created map as a 2D byte array
      Throws:
      RuntimeException - if the map file cannot be read or parsed

    • transferByte

      public static BaseBlockPane transferByte(byte b)
      Converts a byte value to its corresponding block type instance. Byte values map to specific block types:
      • -1 - Slime enemy
      • 0 - Empty/void
      • 1 - Dirt
      • 2 - Grass
      • 3 - Stone
      • 4 - Lava
      • 5 - Dog (objective)
      • 6 - Iron ore
      • 7 - Gold ore
      • 8 - Diamond ore
      • 9 - Wood log
      • 10 - Leaves
      • 11 - Bedrock
      Parameters:
      b - the byte value representing a block type
      Returns:
      a new BaseBlockPane instance of the corresponding type

    • setMap

      public static void setMap(int row, int column, byte type)
      Sets the block type at a specific map position.
      Parameters:
      row - the row position
      column - the column position
      type - the byte value representing the block type

    • getWidth

      public static int getWidth()
      Gets the width (number of columns) of the game map.
      Returns:
      the map width in blocks

    • getHeight

      public static int getHeight()
      Gets the height (number of rows) of the game map.
      Returns:
      the map height in blocks

    • getMap

      public static byte[][] getMap()
      Gets the entire map data as a 2D byte array.
      Returns:
      the map array where each byte represents a block type

    • setMap

      public static void setMap(byte[][] newMap)
      Sets the entire map to a new 2D byte array.
      Parameters:
      newMap - the new map array
